thesis: Best-positioned hyperscaler in the new "show me the FCF" regime: 18% revenue growth at 39% net margin, $627B backlog, Azure +40%, with a ~$190B AI capex program funded 100% from operating cash flow and no equity raise. At 19.6x FY27 earnings with RSI washed out at 18.6 near the 52-week low, the stock has pre-paid most of its macro-driven de-rating; base case $464 (+22%), bull $623 if FY27 capex digestion and Copilot monetization deliver.

Meta has the best P&L in the Mag7 (+33% revenue, 82% gross margin) at one of the lowest forward multiples (17.6x), yet sits ~27% off its high because the market now grades AI on capital intensity, where its $125-145B ad-funded capex and a reported ~$64B equity raise screen worst. The valuation case is compelling if the dilution question resolves benignly (fair value clusters $700-830), but a confirmed mega-raise into a higher-for-longer Fed could re-test the $520 52-week low. Highest-torque, highest-variance bet in the group; next catalysts are the raise decision and July 29 earnings.

Profitable-transition China media name with 13 straight double-digit ad quarters, 15 quarters of gross-margin expansion and ~20% of market cap in net cash, now pinned to its 52-week low at $17.20 (12.8x forward) purely on China-ADR/Hang Seng Tech flows after a clean Q1 beat. The entire bull case is whether the 2H26 game pipeline (NCard July, San Wang/Lumi Master Q4) converts record engagement into gaming EPS. Flow-hostage falling knife with no confirmed bottom; accumulate small only with July's NCard launch as the validation gate, 12-month base case $21.50 (+25%).

GM is the cheapest highly-profitable automaker at 5.6x forward earnings with the legacy cohort's best operating margin (9.4%), ~$10B automotive FCF, and a $6B buyback retiring ~8% of the float, sitting right at its 5-year average multiple after a +66% twelve-month run. The Hormuz oil-spike tail has eased on a holding US-Iran ceasefire and RSI has reset to neutral, but a bearish MACD, ~$50M of late-May C-suite selling at $80-85, Brent above $100, and a hawkish Fed under new Chair Warsh argue for patience on entry. Base-case fair value ~$92 (+16%); buy-on-weakness in the $74-78 zone ahead of the July 21 Q2 print.

LULU has collapsed 51% over the past year to ~$112 (9.1x trailing / ~10x forward earnings, 1.1x sales) after a Q1 FY26 miss and FY26 guidance cut driven by weak US comps (-5% to -6% cc), tariffs (~$240-320M/yr), and share loss to Alo/Vuori. The bull case rests on a 55.7% gross margin, 32% ROE, ~9% FCF yield, +13% China comps, an incoming Nike-veteran CEO, and notable insider buying on the way down. It is a value-trap-vs-generational-buy debate: base case $132 (+18%) requires only stabilization, but 9-10x may be fair value if US decline proves structural.

Highly speculative, essentially pre-revenue quantum-photonics name trading at ~560x sales on $4.3M of mostly-acquired revenue, with negative margins and ~$20M/quarter burn. Real assets are a ~$1B cash pile (a ~$4.35/share floor, but funded by relentless dilution—shares +64% YoY) and a government-adjacent TFLN foundry/NeuraWave narrative riding the 50%+ quantum-sector theme rally; a credible short-seller record (Capybara, Iceberg) and active fraud litigation make disclosure quality itself a risk. Scenario expected value (~$10.40) sits almost exactly at spot—roughly fairly priced for binary risk; venture-style, total-loss-tolerant sizing only.

Broadcom delivered a record Q2 FY26 (+48% revenue, AI semis +143% to $10.8B) and has fully recovered from its June 4 earnings-day crash, trading at $411 — about 21x forward, below its own 5-year average and at a ~33% discount to AI-design peers. It is the highest-margin, most contracted way to own the AI accelerator buildout via named multi-year custom-XPU customers, an Ethernet-for-AI networking franchise, and a ~$29B/yr 90%-margin VMware software annuity, with sell-side raising targets (BofA $530, JPM ~$580) and BofA adding it to an 'AI Big 10.' Base case $465 (+13%), bull $582 (+41%) on an FY27 framework raise; key risk is XPU lumpiness and hyperscaler-financing-driven deployment timing.

NVIDIA at ~$211 trades at 16.6x FY28 (23.5x current-year) earnings — the cheapest forward multiple in its modern history — despite 85% revenue growth, 63% net margins, ~$40B net cash, and a sold-out Blackwell/Rubin roadmap. The June fear regime is easing (Hormuz reopened, oil ~$77, VIX 16, hawkish-but-priced Fed hold), but two real risks keep it Bullish-not-euphoric: China data-center GPU sales remain banned at baseline (the H200 thaw is partial and reversible) and the whole AI cycle now leans on credit markets the Fed is no longer easing into. Base case $280 (+33%) needs only that estimates hold while macro keeps calming.

Tiger Brokers sits at 0.97x book and ~7x normalized earnings after a CSRC mainland-brokerage crackdown that froze its mainland accounts (sell-only) on June 12 and triggered a multi-week analyst PT/estimate slashing (FY26 EPS consensus cut $0.94->$0.41); the headline '5x forward / +73% to PT' is illusory because the denominators are stale and still falling. The ~90% non-mainland franchise (Singapore/HK/ANZ) is genuinely compounding (+28% YoY client assets), ex-penalty profitability is intact, insiders own 24.6%, a $50M buyback is live, and Avenir/Li Lin just raised their stake to 10.9% near the lows. Base case ~$5.50 (+17% on normalized EPS) against a live $3 bear; policyRisk stays SEVERE until the two-year CSRC eradication campaign has a defined boundary.

NIO's turnaround is real — Q1 2026 revenue +112% YoY, gross margin up to 19% from 7.6%, a GAAP-profitable Q4 2025, ~18.8% vehicle margin, and record May deliveries of 37,705 (+62.3% YoY) led by the ONVO ramp — yet the stock trades at just ~0.9x sales on a lingering execution discount. June layered on three fresh negatives that drove a ~12% monthly drop: a hawkish June 17 Fed that re-poisoned China-ADR flows, CEO Li's warning that China auto retail could fall 15-20% in 2026, and management's disclosure of >CNY 10,000/car cost inflation from raw materials and memory chips. Neutral with a long bias: base case $7.00 (+39%), contingent on margins surviving the price war and ADR flows stabilizing.

TSM has round-tripped from the June-10 fear regime to a fresh 52-week high (~$462, +18% on the month), led by an NVIDIA Taiwan-expansion pledge and a +30% May revenue print — confirming its toll-booth grip on the AI buildout (NVDA GPUs, hyperscaler ASICs, and AMD accelerators all route through its fabs) at record 66% gross margins. But the rally closed the gap: at 23.5x forward it now trades above its two biggest customers and into the $473 consensus target, leaving an asymmetry that has turned mildly unfavorable. This is now a momentum/quality hold (base $492, +6%; bull $540, +17%; bear $354, -23%) with the Taiwan geopolitical tail and a hawkish June-17 Fed as the key risks.

WeRide is the broadest international L4 robotaxi franchise (~2,800 vehicles, 40+ cities), with accelerating Uber-partnered EU launches in Madrid and Zurich, trading near its all-time low ($6.35) with ~43% of market cap in cash and ~4 years of runway. But it is a pre-profit China ADR burning >$200M/yr with no breakeven guidance, a persistent delisting/data-security overhang, and lumpy revenue, into a Fed that just flipped to a hike bias. Hold / speculative-accumulate only in small size: base case $9.50 (+50%), bear $4.20, bull $14.

Alibaba is a deeply oversold two-engine story: an accelerating AI/cloud platform (+38% YoY, Qwen) bolted onto a barely-growing, FCF-negative consumption business, now trading at 11.6x forward on a >$75B net-cash balance sheet. The ~20% June drop was driven by the June 8 Pentagon Section 1260H Chinese-Military-Company designation plus a hawkish Fed, China's July-1 outbound-investment rules, and a soft 618 festival. Fundamentally cheap but a falling knife with a fresh sanctions tail: 12-month base case ~$126 (+18%), bull $180, bear $78.

Pony AI is executing operationally (Q1'26 revenue +145%, robotaxi +395%, raised 3,500-vehicle FY26 fleet target, city-wide unit-economics breakeven in Guangzhou and Shenzhen, the only China player driverless-commercial in all four Tier-1 cities) yet the stock sits ~5% above its 52-week low and -65% off its high as a hawkish Warsh-Fed and a persistent China-ADR/policy overhang reprice long-duration, pre-profit names. A fortress balance sheet ($1.07B cash vs $16M debt, ~5-year runway, ~$3.03/share net-cash floor) contains the dilution tail risk, but at ~17x EV/S PONY is still the most expensive name in the AV cohort. Hold/Neutral: base case $13 (+56%), bear $5.50 (-34%), bull $21 (+152%), with the Q2 print (~Aug 18) and the ~Nov founder lockup the decisive events.

Alphabet is the AI capital cycle's marquee test case — the only full-stack player (TPU silicon, Gemini models, Cloud distribution, 2B-user Search) with megacap's best fundamental momentum (Cloud +63%, $460B+ backlog, revenue +22%) and an $84.75B equity war chest anchored by Berkshire. After the June raise it has stabilized at ~$367 with analyst targets being raised (TD Cowen $475), but at 25.3x forward it now pays a small premium over its 5Y-average multiple and carries the most multiple to lose in a hawkish-Fed regime. Hold core / add on weakness: base $377 (+3%), bull $500 (+36%), bear $234 (-36%); the real asymmetry sits in TPU externalization upside vs the multi-front antitrust (search-remedies appeal + AdTech/AdX) downside.

Micron is printing the steepest earnings inflection in semiconductor history on a sold-out HBM/DRAM AI-memory supercycle, trading at just 9.6x forward EPS of $117.95 with all of 2026 HBM output under binding multi-year contracts. But the stock just ran +62% in a month to a $1,134 all-time high, now trades ~17% above its mean analyst PT, and sits at the richest trailing valuation in its history (53.5x P/E, 22x sales) into a binary June 24 print where consensus already exceeds guidance. Verdict: Hold/Trim into strength, wait for a gap-zone retest rather than chase the high — momentum and cyclical-top risk now outweigh the cheap-forward-multiple lure.

Li Auto sits at a fresh 52-week low (~$13.21, -49% YoY) after Q1 2026 confirmed a vehicle-margin collapse to the mid-single digits as the cheaper i6 BEV cannibalized its premium EREV mix; the ~18% June drop was driven by the June 17 hawkish Fed draining China-ADR flows plus capital rotating within the trio out of LI into NIO. The bull case is a leaky balance-sheet floor (~$10.30/ADS net cash, ~78% of market cap) plus a falsifiable margin-recovery guide (L9/L8 refresh, ~10% Q2 gross margin), but the Street is cutting (HSBC to $15.60 Hold; 15 Hold vs 11 Buy) and every prior cheap-with-a-floor call has failed. Verdict: hold into the late-August Q2 margin print.

Costco just printed a near-flawless Q3 FY26 (+11.6% revenue, +9.8% comps, +15% EPS, 92.2% renewal rate) yet has de-rated ~13% on the month to $951, slicing through its 200-day average as the oil-shock gas tailwind faded and a hawkish-Fed 4.45% 10Y pressured its multiple. The slide resets forward P/E to 42x — dead-on its 5-year average rather than a premium — so the world's best retail membership annuity is now fairly (not richly) priced. High-quality defensive hold/accumulate-on-weakness: base case ~$1,040 (+9%), bear $781 on a hike-and-growth-scare, with the $920-956 zone the attractive add level.

XPeng sits at its 52-week low ($13.21, -12% on the month) after a hawkish June Fed discounted its long-duration physical-AI optionality and the sell-side kept cutting targets, even as Q1 showed a record 20.6% gross margin and a net-cash balance sheet. The bull case has migrated from EV growth to robotaxi/humanoid/VW-SoC licensing optionality, but the core auto business shrank 17.6% YoY in Q1; everything now hinges on the management-guided Q2 delivery snap-back (+60% QoQ, 100-106k units) actually landing. Neutral / accumulate-on-evidence: base case ~$16.50 (+25%) against a live ~$7.50 bear case, with the August Q2 print and the Guangzhou robotaxi pilot as the binary evidence gates.

Lemonade has transitioned from a cash-burning growth story to a credible path-to-profitability one: +71% revenue, gross profit +159%, in-force premium $1.33B (+32% YoY, ten straight quarters of accelerating growth), car insurance +60%, and a fourth consecutive quarter of positive adjusted free cash flow, with management guiding to EBITDA profitability in Q4 2026 and full-year 2027 off a net-cash balance sheet. The catch is valuation and execution: at 5.4x sales it is the priciest, still-unprofitable name in its insurtech cohort, so the thesis is a forward promise vulnerable to loss-ratio shocks or any growth wobble. At $58.84 the stock prices the base case almost exactly (~+5% to consensus $62), with a binary spread of roughly -34% bear to +44% bull.

Apple is the structural winner of the anti-capex regime — $101B FCF, zero dilution, a ~$110B buyback — and at WWDC 2026 it doubled down on asset-light AI by renting a custom Google Gemini (~$1B/yr) to ship a working Siri, which is why it held up (-0.3% over the month) as the only Mag7 above both its 50- and 200-day averages. But at ~31x forward (≈88th percentile of its own range) the price already discounts a good outcome, with 16.6% growth decelerating against a -13% industry unit forecast, a beta Siri with no GA date on rented intelligence, fresh 'unsustainable' DRAM/NAND cost warnings, and three of four valuation methods at or below spot. Verdict: Hold as the defensive Mag7 allocation, don't add at 31x; base $300 (dead money), bull $355 (+19%), bear $209 (-30%).

Tesla is the Mag7's purest narrative-vs-numbers trade: a 160x-forward multiple priced on robotaxi/FSD/Optimus optionality against an automaker's 4.2% operating margin and ~$5.25B FCF on a $1.50T cap. The mid-June bounce to $400 (+5.3% over 3M) repaired the selloff, but the June 17 hawkish Fed dot-plot flip pressures exactly this long-duration profile, and no cash-flow scenario reaches the current price (bull DCF ~$287). Hold/trim — base case ~$400 (fairly valued), bear $212, bull $717 only on driverless-at-scale; size as a venture sleeve, not core.

Seagate is the prime beneficiary of the AI-datacenter mass-capacity storage supercycle, with HAMR/Mozaic cost leadership, 44% revenue growth, 36% operating margins, and nearline capacity sold out through CY27 at rising $/TB. But after a ~+712% one-year run to an all-time high of $1,070, the stock trades at 39.5x forward / 102x trailing earnings and ~19% above the average analyst target of $898 while the entire C-suite sold into the spike. We rate it Neutral: a great business whose price already discounts the optimistic supercycle scenario, with base-case fair value of ~$677-830 and WDC a cheaper way to own the same thesis.

AMD is the only at-scale alternative to NVDA in AI accelerators, with contracted multi-year demand (OpenAI 6GW, Meta up to 6GW, Oracle 50,000 MI450 GPUs) and Data Center now >57% of revenue growing +57% YoY. But after a +30% month back to within 2% of its all-time high, it trades at 41x FY27 / 73x FY26 earnings while every triangulated fair value (mean reversion, peer parity, DCF, consensus PT $488) sits below the $537 price. Verdict: Trim/Hold, do not chase into a hawkish Fed and a momentum run; accumulate on pullbacks toward the 50DMA.

QCOM has round-tripped +16% on the month back to ~$226 on AI-data-center optimism into its June 24 Investor Day, but the rebound is event-anticipation rather than an earnings inflection — revenue is contracting (-3.5% YoY), FY26 EPS is flat-to-down, and the 21x forward multiple sits at the top of its 5-year band with the analyst mean target ($184) roughly 19% BELOW the price. Our model scores it 40/100 (Bearish): the entire bull case (Dragonfly AI200/AI250, ByteDance ASIC, record +38% automotive) is contingent on a single binary catalyst that already burned momentum buyers -23% once this month. Avoid chasing; base case $200 (-12%), with ~46% China/Huawei exposure and the March-2027 Apple license cliff as structural drags.

Intel has ripped ~21% to a record-close ~$134 on two unconfirmed catalysts — a reported Google 3M+ TPU foundry order (June 8) and a June 18 Trump Truth Social post naming Apple as a foundry customer — yet the fundamentals still show $174M of external foundry revenue, a $2.4B/quarter foundry loss, and -$8.3B TTM FCF at 86.6x forward earnings and 12.5x sales. Consensus PT ($94) now sits ~30% below spot and our model scores it 15/100 (Bearish); the foundry-duopoly option is real but, at the 52-week high on headlines companies have not confirmed and into a hawkish Fed, the reward-to-risk has inverted. Verdict: Hold tilting to Trim — wait for a $100-110 accumulation zone rather than chase the record high; policy risk moderate (US gov ~9.9% stake plus warrant, CHIPS dependence).
